projects.title: High-Voltage EV Battery Pack (900V) — Ohmitron, Inc
projects.subtitle: HV Li-ion battery pack architecture, thermal runaway mitigation, structural & thermal CAE
what: Development of a ~900V high-voltage battery pack for heavy-duty/commercial EVs, focusing on manufacturable architecture and safety-oriented thermal control.
how: CAD + packaging in SolidWorks; module/cell arrangement; sheet metal/weldment structure.
Applied GD&T for manufacturability; harness/cabling and BMS integration workstream using SolidWorks Electrical (as described).
ANSYS-based structural/vibration/thermal/CFD analyses for air/liquid/immersion cooling and thermal runaway scenarios (as described).
results: Claimed reduction of charging time to ~20 minutes at 10C-rate (portfolio claim).
projects.evidenceNote: Some results are stated as claims in the portfolio; if you have public patent numbers, test reports, or release-ready proofs, add them here.

projects.title: Modular EV DC Fast Charger (120–360kW) — Ohmitron, Inc
projects.subtitle: Scalable cabinet design + airflow/thermal optimization
what: Designed a convertible modular fast-charger cabinet supporting multiple power outputs via add/remove converter modules.
Compatibility stated for CCS1 and NACS with an intelligent controller.
how: Sheet metal cabinet modeling in SolidWorks with GD&T; DFA/DFM-informed iteration.
Thermal/airflow CFD + structural analysis in ANSYS for robustness and cooling efficiency.
Electrical cabling/layout via SolidWorks Electrical; integration with EE/SW teams.
results: Standardized modular architecture around a 120kW base (portfolio claim).
Reported optimal internal temperatures ~55–65°C at max load (portfolio claim).
projects.evidenceNote: If any field data, test logs, or compliance standards are public, add citations here.

projects.title: Automotive-Grade 12V Li-ion Battery — Ohmitron, Inc
projects.subtitle: Vibration/impact robustness + extreme-cold operation (portfolio claim)
what: Development of an automotive-grade 12V Li-ion battery design (4 prismatic cells, 3.2V each) adaptable across multiple ampere ratings.
Enclosure target IP65; stated suitability across vehicles and stationary storage.
how: Modularized the core mechanical design; engineered casing and cell holders for loads/vibration/impact and BMS mounts with DFA/DFM + GD&T.
Thermal management using heating/cooling pads; CAD in SolidWorks.
ANSYS analyses: FEA/modal/frequency, thermal CFD, and impact (portfolio description).
results: Extreme-cold operation claimed down to -40°C using thermal pads and advanced cells (portfolio claim).
Fatigue/design-life claims for cell holders (portfolio claim).
projects.evidenceNote: For PhD audiences, replace or back up superlatives (e.g., “almost infinite life”) with quantified fatigue cycles, safety factors, and assumptions.

projects.title: ASME E-Fests XRC Autonomous Vehicle — 1st Place (2023 & 2024)
projects.subtitle: Chassis design + crash/FEA scenarios + aerodynamic/weight optimization
what: Designed competition vehicles (Ariel Atom 2023; Mercedes F1-inspired 2024) within strict size/weight constraints for autonomous racetrack challenge.
how: Chassis CAD in SolidWorks using weldments; surface modeling for aero components; simulation for crash scenarios (portfolio description).
Iterated for low weight and reduced drag.
results: 1st place worldwide twice; reported finish times 15s (2023) and 13s (2024) (portfolio claim).
Reported ~15% speed gain vs others via drag/weight reduction (portfolio claim).
projects.evidenceNote: If there’s an official ASME results page or certificate link, add it here.

projects.title: Aerospace CAD: Honeywell GTCP APU Models — DUNIT SOL
projects.subtitle: High-fidelity surface/detail modeling from reference imagery
what: Created detailed 3D CAD models of GTCP 36-150RJ and GTCP 131-9 APU units from references and constraints (portfolio description).
how: Translated reference images into detailed CAD assemblies; enforced dimension and aspect constraints and functional details.
results: Reported ~90% fidelity to original hardware (portfolio claim).
projects.evidenceNote: For academic rigor, describe the validation method for “90% fidelity” (e.g., dimensional checks vs known measurements).

projects.title: Hybrid Tire Technology vs Non‑Pneumatic Tires (NPTs)
projects.subtitle: FEA-based design verification and comparative analysis (paper submitted per CV)
what: Proposed and analyzed a hybrid tire concept aiming to combine puncture-free benefits with improved comfort/weight (portfolio description).
how: CAD in SolidWorks; performed static structural and dynamic analyses; applied representative boundary conditions (e.g., load, gravity, pressure) as described in portfolio.
Material selection included polyurethane composite considerations (portfolio description).
results: Claimed superiority over NPTs, including ~30% weight reduction and better impact integrity (portfolio claim).
projects.evidenceNote: Add the paper PDF/DOI once accepted; include mesh details, convergence notes, and assumptions for reviewers.

projects.title: GFRP Composite Insulators for HV Power Transmission (Thesis / Industry R&D)
projects.subtitle: Design, manufacturing line setup, experimental validation + CAE correlation
what: Design and development of non-ceramic GFRP composite insulators for overhead HV line applications (per CV/portfolio).
how: Set up pultrusion and hand lay-up manufacturing lines; performed sampling & lab testing (tensile/bending/electrical/hydrophobic) and standards-oriented validation (per CV).
Validated and modeled via FEA and composite material modeling in Abaqus (per CV).
results: Claimed improvements over ceramic insulators (e.g., longer life, reduced incidents) stated in portfolio; add quantified test results for credibility.
projects.evidenceNote: For PhD reviewers: include test matrix, standards used, sample sizes, and a clear comparison baseline.
